_meta.description: Single source of truth for color, spacing, type and radius used across the Cartrack Fleet Portal (fleet-web). Every value below was read out of the actual codebase, not an idealized spec — where a value is computed rather than literal, the formula is noted.
generatedFrom: libs/shared/js/karoo-ui/core/src/lib/ThemeProvider.tsx (current system — MUI v9 theme, all new UI)
apps/fleet-web/src/shared/components/styled/global-styles/*.ts (legacy pre-MUI layer, still imported in ~119 files)
_meta.structure: primitive = raw values with no meaning attached. semantic = named roles components/agents should actually reference (aliases into primitive, written as {path.to.value}). legacy = the older styled-components layer, kept for traceability while it's phased out.
_meta.rule: Always reference semantic.* (e.g. semantic.color.brand.primary.main), never a primitive value or raw hex, directly in a component.
changelog: 2026-07-16: wired tokens.json into all 27 component-repo components; surfaced and closed 4 recurring gaps found in the process — see typography.scale.{labelSmall,titleMedium}, color.surface.inverse, and the new borderWidth category below. Each addition's note explains what's real vs. gap-filling.

2026-07-16: fixed semantic.typography.fontFamily — it was pointing at the legacy Roboto stack (no Helvetica/Arial fallback) instead of MUI's real unoverridden default; added primitive.fontFamily.muiDefault and repointed it. Found while syncing component-library-preview.html to this file.

2026-07-16: synced component-library-preview.html's hardcoded :root CSS variables to match this file exactly (--bg, --selected were off), and fixed two real WCAG AA contrast bugs the preview was silently demonstrating (Button's contained fill, Picker's header) despite its own Do/Don't text already warning against them.

2026-07-20: added usage notes to semantic token groups (brand, text, surface, vehicleStatus, spacing, radius) so every group explains when to use it — Polaris-style per-token guidance, group-level granularity. Added 'related' (name + why) to all 27 component doc.jsons and created templates/ with 5 page patterns (table-page, detail-page, map-tracking-view, settings-page, login).

2026-07-23: added semantic.icon (sizing/colour story) and components/Icon/Icon.doc.json — closes the Icon gap flagged in foundations/iconography.html. Corrected primitive.fontFamily.fontAwesome's note: it's a near-dead legacy webfont reference (2 files), not the app's real icon system, which is @fortawesome/react-fontawesome (Free+Pro tiers) via the Icon wrapper component. Also fixed stale 'Material icon ligature' prop descriptions on Button.leadingIcon and TextField.leading/trailingIcon.

2026-07-23: added primitive.color.status.{success,warning,error}.{50,900} and semantic.color.statusChip — the status-chip tint pairs (Active/Idle/Offline) previously existed only as repeated literals in component-library-preview.html (.schip) and demo code; Chip's live styling now binds them. info deliberately excluded: no status-chip mapping uses it.

2026-07-23: added primitive.color.status.info.{50,900} and semantic.color.status.*.tint (50-tone surface fills) — Banner's severity backgrounds were documented in its header comment but existed nowhere; statusChip backgrounds alias the same 50-tone primitives.

2026-07-24: component library swapped from the MDC-modeled set to components/ built on real MUI v9 (@karoo-ui/core parity). tokens.json is unchanged structurally, but the semantic tier's runtime consumer is now components/theme.ts (createTheme built from these values) — the resolved-literal exceptions story in AGENTS.md rule 3 was superseded by theme-based styling. Old→new component name mapping: components/README.md.

hue.note: Categorical/data-viz palette from _variables.scss. 'base' is the named Sass variable (e.g. $red); numbered steps are tint()/shade() of base — lower number = lighter tint, higher = darker shade. Only steps that actually exist in source are listed (the scale isn't uniform across hues).

surface.note: Gap-filling addition (2026-07-16): fleet-web has no MD3-style inverse-surface role, but Snackbar and Tooltip both independently need a dark surface for a floating high-contrast overlay on an otherwise all-light-surface app. inverse is Snackbar's own literal spec value (component-specs/snackbars.md → Snackbar.doc.json), reused here as the one canonical dark surface instead of letting Tooltip define a second, slightly different grey.

borderWidth.note: Gap-filling addition (2026-07-16): hairline/stroke widths are a different concern from layout spacing and don't belong on the 4px spacing grid (a '1px' or '2px' spacing step would break the n*4 grid math for actual layout use). Split out as its own primitive category instead.

spacing.note: 4px steps. MUI's theme.spacing(n) = n * 8px, so e.g. primitive.spacing.2 = theme.spacing(1) and primitive.spacing.4 = theme.spacing(2).

radius.note: MUI default shape — never overridden in ThemeProvider.tsx (theme.shape.borderRadius = 4).

fontFamily.note: roboto/robotoCondensed/hermes/fontAwesome are the LEGACY styled-components stack (apps/fleet-web/.../global-styles/typography.ts) — bare 'Roboto, sans-serif', no fallback chain. muiDefault is the CURRENT karoo-ui/MUI stack: ThemeProvider.tsx never overrides typography.fontFamily, so MUI's real unoverridden default applies (confirmed in node_modules/@mui/material/styles/createTypography.js). These are genuinely different strings — semantic.typography.fontFamily below correctly points at muiDefault, not roboto. CORRECTION (2026-07-23): 'fontAwesome' here is the legacy CSS webfont-ligature reference and is almost dead — only 2 files in the whole app still use it (both map-view Resizer grip glyphs). It is NOT the app's real icon system. The real, dominant icon system is @fortawesome/react-fontawesome (SVG components, Free+Pro tiers, forked under src/_third-party-libs-forks), registered once in font-initialization.ts and consumed everywhere through the Icon wrapper (src/components/Icon) — see the new semantic.icon group below and components/Icon/Icon.doc.json. Kept this primitive as-is for traceability; just don't cite it as 'the icon font'.

brand.note: White-label: primary/secondary are read from user/client settings at runtime (KarooUiCustomizableTheme). Reference the role, never assume the hex — values below are the Cartrack default theme only.

status.note: Fixed — not white-label customizable. Status only; never use for brand or decorative emphasis.

text.note: The only text colours for content on light surfaces. primary for body/headings, secondary for supporting text, disabled for inactive. Never use raw black.

surface.note: What things sit on: default/paper for components, appBackground behind pages, inverse (+onInverse) for dark surfaces like snackbars and tooltips.

border.note: Systemic border tokens only. One-off component borders (e.g. ToggleButtonGroup's #BDBDBD, set directly in ThemeProvider.tsx's MuiToggleButtonGroup override) belong in that component's own <Name>.doc.json under 'tokens', not here — don't grow this section into a dumping ground for single-use values.

interactive.note: Computed in ThemeProvider.tsx as theme.palette.states.<primary|secondary>.<hover|selected|focus> = alpha(brand color, MUI action opacity: hover 0.04 / selected 0.08 / focus 0.12). Prefer these over inventing new alpha values.

dataViz.note: Categorical palette for charts, tags, and map/report colour-coding. Restructured to match Astryx's per-hue token shape (background/border/icon/text — 4 roles picked BY FUNCTION, not by lightness) instead of a generic light/mid/dark ladder. Caveat: unlike Astryx's OKLCH-generated ramps, fleet-web's Sass tint()/shade() steps are narrow (a single ~10% tint and a few shades off one base hue, not a full pastel-to-saturated range) — so 'background' here is a mild tint, not a soft pastel, and several hues re-use the same step for icon/text where no distinct darker shade exists in source (flagged per hue below).

orange.note: no 70 step in source — icon reuses text (80).

yellow.note: no 70 step in source — icon reuses text (80).

lime.note: no 70 step in source — icon reuses text (80).

green.note: no 70 step in source — icon reuses text (80).

pink.note: no 80 step in source — darkest available (70) used for text.

gray.note: background (gray.10) is the same value as semantic.color.surface.appBackground — expect low contrast against the page canvas; consider gray.20 for anything that needs to read as a distinct chip/badge.

vehicleStatus.note: Fixed fleet/map iconography colours — not part of the general palette, don't reuse for unrelated UI.

statusChip.note: Read-only status chip tint pairs (Active=success, Idle=warning, Offline=error) — the mapping used by Chip variant=status, the preview's .schip classes, and DataTable status cells. 50-tone fill + 900-tone text passes AA.

spacing.note: MUI 8px grid. Use sx={{ p: 2 }} / theme.spacing(2) — never arbitrary px.

radius.note: Never overridden — theme.shape.borderRadius = 4 everywhere (buttons, cards, dialogs, inputs). Chip uses MUI's own Chip default (16px), not the theme shape value.

borderWidth.note: Gap-filling addition (2026-07-16) — see primitive.borderWidth. hairline is for a genuine 1px rule (Divider); thin is for a slightly heavier 2px stroke (e.g. an inactive slider track).

typography.note: Roboto scale at a 14px base. theme.typography.htmlFontSize = 14 AND html { font-size: 14px } (apps/fleet-web/src/styles/base/_base.scss) are kept in sync, so these render at exactly the px shown below, not scaled up/down. Default text variant app-wide is body2 (set via MuiTypography defaultProps).

labelSmall.note: Gap-filling addition (2026-07-16) — Badge's count, Chip's status-chip label, and NavigationRail's label all independently spec'd a 12px/500 combination that doesn't exist in MUI's real classic scale (its 12px step, caption, is 400-weight). Named using MD3 type-role conventions since MD2/MUI-classic has no name for this size+weight pairing; lineHeight matches caption, the same-size sibling already in this scale. IMPORTANT: this is NOT a valid MUI Typography `variant` prop today — using it requires extending karoo-ui's theme.typography with a custom variant (and the matching TS module augmentation), which hasn't been done. Until then, treat this as the target spec, not something you can pass as variant="labelSmall".

titleMedium.note: Gap-filling addition (2026-07-16) — Card's title, ExpansionPanel's header, and SideSheet's header title all independently spec'd 16px/500, which doesn't exist in MUI's real classic scale (16px is subtitle1 at 400-weight; 500-weight starts at h6's 20px). Named using MD3 type-role conventions for the same reason as labelSmall above; lineHeight matches subtitle1, the same-size sibling. Same caveat: NOT a valid MUI Typography `variant` prop until karoo-ui's theme is extended.

icon.note: Closes the gap flagged in foundations/iconography.html (2026-07-22 stub). Added 2026-07-23 alongside components/Icon/Icon.doc.json. This documents the real mechanisms already in the codebase rather than inventing a new pixel scale or colour token — a scan of real <Icon> usage in fleet-web found no consistent size convention to extract (explicit size props are rare and inconsistent: 5 instances of size="lg", nothing else), so there is nothing to promote to a semantic scale yet.

sizing.note: No dedicated icon-size token exists. Icons size via FontAwesomeIcon's own built-in `size` prop — a fixed enum, not a token — or inherit the surrounding font-size like any inline glyph.

colour.note: No dedicated icon-colour token exists either. Icons default to currentColor (no `color` prop passed) — reuse the existing text-colour roles for standard on-surface icons, or the categorical hue.<hue>.icon roles above for status/tag-coded icons. Don't invent a new icon-specific colour token; these already cover it.

legacy.note: Pre-MUI styled-components layer (apps/fleet-web/src/shared/components/styled/global-styles). Still imported in ~119 files, so it's real and load-bearing — but don't build new UI against it. New components should use semantic.* above; fleet-web is migrating toward the karoo-ui/MUI system.

spacing.note: Different base unit than the current system (5px, not 4/8px) — do not mix the two scales in the same layout.

fontWeight.note: INCONSISTENCY, confirmed in source: legacy 'bold' is 600, current/MUI 'bold' is 700. Same name, different value — check which layer a component is in before reusing a weight token.

breakpoints.note: Custom em-based breakpoints, not MUI's default px breakpoints (xs:0/sm:600/md:900/lg:1200/xl:1536). Outside this file's stated scope (color/spacing/type/radius), flagged here only because it's the same kind of value duplication risk.

knownIssues: White text/icons (onPrimary) on semantic.color.brand.primary.main (#F47735) is ~2.79:1 contrast — fails WCAG AA for normal text. For text-bearing fills, use brand.primary.dark (#BB4800, ~5.2:1) or dark text on the orange surface (~7.5:1) instead.
